title: Pangolakha Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Pangolakha Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Sikkim

overview.short: Pangolakha Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary encompassing an area of 124 km2 (48 sq mi) in the Pakyong District of the Indian state of Sikkim.
overview.description: Pangolakha Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary encompassing an area of 124 km2 (48 sq mi) in the Pakyong District of the Indian state of Sikkim. It was established in 2002 and includes the hamlets of Aritar, Dakline Lingtam, Phadamchen, Dzuluk, Gnathang Monastery Kupup. It is about 28 km (17 mi) east of Rorathang and about 40 km (25 mi) by road from Rangpo city.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Royal Monetary Authority of Bhutan
topPlaces.description: The Royal Monetary Authority of Bhutan (Dzongkha: རྒྱལ་གཞུང་དངུལ་ལས་དབང་འཛིན་) is the central bank of Bhutan and is a member of the Asian Clearing Union. It is also the minting authority for the Bhutanese Ngultrum.

topPlaces.name: Lake Tsomgo
topPlaces.description: Tsomgo Lake, also known as Tsongmo Lake or Changgu Lake, is a glacial lake in Changu in the Gangtok district of the Indian state of Sikkim, some 40 kilometres (25 mi) from the capital Gangtok. Located at an elevation of 3,753 m (12,313 ft), the lake remains frozen during the winter season.

topPlaces.name: Kyongnosla Alpine Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Kyongnosla Alpine Sanctuary is a nature reserve in Gangtok district, Sikkim, India. It is situated around the area adjoining Lake Tsomgo and covers an area of about 31 km2 (12 sq mi).

topPlaces.name: Lake Menmecho
topPlaces.description: Menmecho Lake is a mountain lake located in Gangtok district in the Indian state of Sikkim. It lies on the way to the Jelep Pass and is situated 20 kilometres (12 mi) ahead of the Lake Tsomgo.

topPlaces.name: Sherathang
topPlaces.description: Sherathang is a town in Gangtok district near the Nathu La Pass in Sikkim, India. The location has been identified as the site for excise, customs and checking for trade between India and China.

topPlaces.name: Nathang Valley
topPlaces.description: Nathang Valley (also spelt as Natang, Gnathong, Gnathang Valley) Tibetan Himalayan Platue at 4932 meter, 92 KM from Gangtok is located in Pakyong District, Sikkim of India. The Nathang Valley is part of the Gnathang-Machong Vidhan Sabha constituency of the Sikkim Legislative Assembly.As per the 2011 census of India, Gnathang village covers 16 hectares housing 13 households with a total population of 60 (29 males, 31 females).

topPlaces.name: Kupup
topPlaces.description: Kupup is a hamlet in the Indian state of Sikkim near the border with China. It lies in a transverse valley below the Dongkya Range, close to the Jelep La pass.

topPlaces.name: Sikkim expedition
topPlaces.description: The Sikkim expedition (Chinese: 隆吐山戰役; pinyin: Lóngtǔ Shān Zhànyì; lit. 'Battle of the Longtu Mountain') was an 1888 British military expedition to expel Tibetan forces from Sikkim.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Royal Monetary Authority of Bhutan & around

items.note: The Royal Monetary Authority of Bhutan (Dzongkha: རྒྱལ་གཞུང་དངུལ་ལས་དབང་འཛིན་) is the central bank of Bhutan and is a member of the Asian Clearing Union. It is also the minting authority for the Bhutanese Ngultrum.

Tsomgo Lake, also known as Tsongmo Lake or Changgu Lake, is a glacial lake in Changu in the Gangtok district of the Indian state of Sikkim, some 40 kilometres (25 mi) from the capital Gangtok. Located at an elevation of 3,753 m (12,313 ft), the lake remains frozen during the winter season.
Kyongnosla Alpine Sanctuary is a nature reserve in Gangtok district, Sikkim, India. It is situated around the area adjoining Lake Tsomgo and covers an area of about 31 km2 (12 sq mi).
Menmecho Lake is a mountain lake located in Gangtok district in the Indian state of Sikkim. It lies on the way to the Jelep Pass and is situated 20 kilometres (12 mi) ahead of the Lake Tsomgo.
Sherathang is a town in Gangtok district near the Nathu La Pass in Sikkim, India. The location has been identified as the site for excise, customs and checking for trade between India and China.

Nathang Valley (also spelt as Natang, Gnathong, Gnathang Valley) Tibetan Himalayan Platue at 4932 meter, 92 KM from Gangtok is located in Pakyong District, Sikkim of India. The Nathang Valley is part of the Gnathang-Machong Vidhan Sabha constituency of the Sikkim Legislative Assembly.As per the 2011 census of India, Gnathang village covers 16 hectares housing 13 households with a total population of 60 (29 males, 31 females).
Kupup is a hamlet in the Indian state of Sikkim near the border with China. It lies in a transverse valley below the Dongkya Range, close to the Jelep La pass.
The Sikkim expedition (Chinese: 隆吐山戰役; pinyin: Lóngtǔ Shān Zhànyì; lit. 'Battle of the Longtu Mountain') was an 1888 British military expedition to expel Tibetan forces from Sikkim.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pangolakha Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pangolakha Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pangolakha Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pakyong Airport, Gangtok (21 km) and the nearest railway station is Siliguri Junction (77 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pangolakha Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pangolakha Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pangolakha Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Pangolakha Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pangolakha Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Royal Monetary Authority of Bhutan, Lake Tsomgo, Kyongnosla Alpine Sanctuary, Lake Menmecho, Sherathang.
